violations.text: Reduced Oxygen Packaged Lobster was observed held in refrigeration in its intact packaging at 33-34 degrees Fahrenheit. REDUCED OXYGEN PACKAGED FISH/SEAFOOD that bears a label indicating that it is to be kept frozen until time of use shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to its thawing under refrigeration. Proper seafood thawing guidance was provided to the PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026

Drinking water bottles were observed used as ice sticks/wands in chowder. EQUIPMENT/UTENSILS used for cooling of soups/chowders shall be designed and constructed to be durable for the intended normal use conditions. PIC to obtain proper cooling ice wands meant for proper sanitizing and multi-use applications. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026
The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

ROP Lobster and mahi tuna was observed thawing with the package in tact. The lobster was observed at 35 degrees and was sliced open. The mahi tuna was observed at 53 degrees with no explanation. Tuna was disposed of. All ROP seafood must be thawed with package not in tact to allow the introduction of oxygen. Owner to instruct staff of proper thawing procedures of ROP seafood. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation of bio-film. Owner stated machine would be turned off and wash rinsed and sanitized. Inspector recommends increasing cleaning frequency of ice machine to prevent build up. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023
